title: Exported-API JSDoc + code-comments assert false .project/ resolution paths, contradicting pointer-resolved code and the FGAP-108-corrected READMEs
description: Surfaced by the FGAP-108 fresh-context audit. Several exported-API JSDoc blocks (and module-level / interface doc-comments) document substrate discovery/resolution paths as literal `.project/...` tier-lists — e.g. agent-spec.ts:210 and types.ts:96 ('1. {cwd}/.project/agents/{name}.agent.yaml'), template.ts:6 ('1. {cwd}/.project/templates/'), behavior-monitors/index.ts:190-192 ('block-api derives the on-disk path as <cwd>/.project/<block>.json'). The runtime code is POINTER-RESOLVED (verified: agent-spec.ts/template.ts use tryResolveContextDir; compile.ts uses schemaPath; both from @davidorex/pi-context/context-dir; no hardcoded `.project`), so the JSDoc is factually wrong AND now directly contradicts the FGAP-108-corrected README (pi-jit-agents/README.md:31 says `{cwd}/<substrate-dir>/agents/...`). This is the developer-API-doc surface (IDE hover / generated API docs), distinct from the agent-facing + end-user-README surfaces FGAP-106/108 fixed (those were deliberately scoped to LEAVE JSDoc). Same .project-as-canonical mis-teaching class, different audience. NOT a code bug — code is correct; only the docs lag.

evidence.reference: createAgentLoader JSDoc tier-list '1. {cwd}/.project/agents/{name}.agent.yaml' — code uses tryResolveContextDir (agent-spec.ts:226)
LoadContext JSDoc teaches .project/agents/, .project/<name>.json, .project/schemas/<name>.schema.json — pointer-resolved in reality; contradicts README:31
MonitorAction JSDoc 'block-api derives the on-disk path as <cwd>/.project/<block>.json' — block-api is pointer-resolved

JSDoc describing block-api as operating on .project/*.json — illustrative/historical; resolution is pointer-driven. Enumerate exhaustively (the audit found a subset; ~30+ .project JSDoc lines across packages).

impact: A developer reading the exported pi-jit-agents / pi-behavior-monitors / pi-context API (IDE hover or generated docs) is taught that discovery/writes go to a fixed `.project/` dir — false per DEC-0015 (pointer-resolved, user-chosen dir) and contradicting the corrected READMEs. Low runtime impact (docs only) but a real internal contradiction introduced by FGAP-108's README fixes.
canonical_vocabulary: API documentation accuracy / single-source-of-truth for resolution-path docs
proposed_resolution: Exhaustively enumerate (partitioned grep, pure enumeration) every JSDoc/code-comment across all packages that asserts a `.project/` resolution or discovery path (tier-lists, 'derives the on-disk path as', 'reads .project/*.json', 'resolves to .project/schemas/...'). Converge to the pointer-resolved truth: reference resolveContextDir / the substrate-dir (e.g. '<substrate-dir>/agents/' or 'the resolved substrate dir') rather than the literal `.project`. Distinguish factually-wrong path-assertions (FIX) from incidental this-repo/historical mentions. Keep code unchanged. Verify: zero JSDoc `.project`-as-resolution-path outside an explicit LEAVE set; no README/JSDoc contradiction remains. Same DEC-0018 gate where applicable (build/check/test; this is comment-only so no runtime demo needed beyond grep + audit).
